title: ferrotorch-core: hardsigmoid f64 backward gradient is more precise than PyTorch reference — surfaced by phase 2.5 (#767)
description: Phase 2.5 conformance probe shows ferrotorch's hardsigmoid f64 gradient = 1/6 in f64 (0.16666666666666666) while PyTorch's f64 reference = 0.1666666716337204 (which is 1/6 rounded at f32 precision then cast back). PyTorch's hardsigmoid_backward apparently uses an f32 constant internally even for f64 inputs. Ferrotorch is correct; PyTorch's is the divergence. To close: relax the hardsigmoid_with grad fixture-comparison tolerance to absorb the documented PyTorch f32 round-trip, OR document this as a 'ferrotorch is more precise than PyTorch' divergence.
